_comment: Plan 2 T14 — Pathfinder archetype dialog. Three archetypes: lone_wolf, talkative, superstitious. Loaded by CompanionDialogueCatalog. Mirrors companion_sergeant.json structure. Token usage: {PLAYER_NAME} for player by name; {PLAYER_RANK} for the player's culture-appropriate rank title (RankHelper); {LORD_NAME} for the enlisted lord; {COMPANION_NAME}/{COMPANION_FIRST_NAME} when player options address the companion. Tokens are populated by EnlistedMenuBehavior.SetCompanionConversationTokens before the conversation opens.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: You're the new one. Stay out of my line of sight when I'm reading the ground.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Ho there, {PLAYER_NAME}! New face — and a {PLAYER_RANK}, no less. Tell me, has anyone shown you the trick of reading deer-tracks in soft mud yet? No? Well, sit down — we've a whole afternoon and a fire that wants tending.

options.text: Show me, {COMPANION_FIRST_NAME}. I'd like to learn.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A new soul under this sky. The crows watched you ride in, {PLAYER_NAME} — three of them, on the south fence. Three is a kind number. The omens point at you. You'll do well, if you mind the signs.

options.text: I'll mind them. What other signs should I watch for, {COMPANION_NAME}?
Crows are crows. Tracks are what matter.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Good. Quiet rider lives longer than a loud one. Find me when you need a route.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Splendid, {PLAYER_NAME}! Stick near and I'll teach you everything I picked up from a dozen seasons in the high country. Half of it might even be true. A {PLAYER_RANK} who can read terrain — that's rare and worth keeping.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The land speaks to those who listen, {PLAYER_NAME}. Walk gently and don't whistle after sundown — that one's older than the kingdoms.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Looking for something out there?

options.text: What does the country look like ahead?
How did the last fight look from your saddle?
options.tooltip: Recent battle from a scout's view

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Wind from the north. Ground's hard. Two streams east of the column, both fordable. That's all you need.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Oh, the country here is grand, {PLAYER_NAME}! Rolling hills like an old quilt, a copse of birch on the west side that shelters a fox den, and the wind's been swinging east since dawn — rain by tomorrow, mark me. Tell {LORD_NAME} to oil the bowstrings tonight.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The land's restless, {PLAYER_NAME}. Old cairn west of the road — don't camp inside its shadow. The clouds came in from the wrong quarter this morning. Something is unsettled.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A column of riders, four miles. Not banners I recognize. Tracks suggest they passed at first light. Move or wait — your choice, {PLAYER_RANK}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Funny you should ask, {PLAYER_NAME} — I rode the ridge at dawn and saw the prettiest valley you ever did see, smoke from a hamlet on the far side, a herd of sheep grazing the slope, and what I'm fairly sure was a pair of bandit scouts pretending not to watch the road. Three threats and one breakfast spot, all in one ride.

options.text: Good ride. I'll relay it to {LORD_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: The hawks would not come down to the ridge. That tells me men are camped in the hollow beyond it — hawks know better than maps. Two fires, I'd wager, by the smell of the smoke at dawn. The omens point at you, {PLAYER_NAME} — your eyes were meant to see this.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Their flank was open from the rise. {LORD_NAME} saw it late. Cost us time, not lives. Could have been worse.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Now THAT was a sight from the saddle, {PLAYER_NAME}! I was up on the eastern hummock when their horse swung wide — could see the whole field like a tabletop. Reminded me of a scrap I watched in the Battanian hills six years back, only this time we had the high ground, the better archers, and {LORD_NAME} for once didn't dither at the wrong moment.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: A raven sat on the rotten oak before the fight. I marked it and rode wide. The men I rode with came back. The men I didn't, did not. Heed the trees, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Eat before you ride. Water your horse before yourself. And never trust a road you can't see the end of.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Talk to the locals, {PLAYER_NAME}. Always! Shepherds know more than scouts half the time, and an old woman by a well will save your column three days of wandering for the price of a kind word and a copper. Even a {PLAYER_RANK} can buy that much intelligence on the cheap.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Greet the bridges before you cross them. Leave a crust at the cairn when you pass one. The wilderness does not forget a kindness, nor a slight, {PLAYER_NAME}.

nodes.speaker: companion
nodes.text: Ride light, {PLAYER_NAME}. Eyes on the horizon.
